sn22.85:1.1: At one time, venerable Sāriputta was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
sn22.85:1.2: Now at that time, an evil view had arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Yamaka:
sn22.85:1.3: “As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:10.2: do you regard form [*rūpaṁ*] … *vedanā* … *saññā* … *saṅkhāras* … *viññāṇa* together as the Tathāgata?”
sn22.85:11.2: do you regard this one who is formless … without *vedanā* … without *saññā* … without *saṅkhāras* … without *viññāṇa* as the Tathāgata?”
sn22.85:11.4: “And since, friend Yamaka, the Tathāgata is not found here in this very life as true and established, is it proper for you to make that declaration:
sn22.85:11.5: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:12.1: “Previously, friend Sāriputta, when I was ignorant, I had that evil view;
sn22.85:12.2: but now, having heard this teaching of *dhamma* from venerable Sāriputta, that evil view has been abandoned, and the *dhamma* has been comprehended by me.”
sn22.85:13.1: “Friend Yamaka, if they should ask you thus:
sn22.85:13.15: what is *dukkha* has ceased and come to an end.’
sn22.85:13.16: Asked in this way, friend, I would answer thus.”
sn22.85:13.2: ‘Friend Yamaka, that *bhikkhu* who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*] — what happens to him with the breakup of the body, after death?’
sn22.85:13.3: Asked in this way, friend Yamaka, how would you answer?”
sn22.85:13.4: “Friend, if they should ask me thus:
sn22.85:13.5: ‘Friend Yamaka, that *bhikkhu* who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*] — what happens to him with the breakup of the body, after death?’
sn22.85:13.6: Asked in this way, friend, I would answer thus:
sn22.85:13.9: what is *dukkha* has ceased and come to an end.
sn22.85:14.10: What if I were to infiltrate and deprive him of life?’
sn22.85:14.11: He would approach that householder or householder's son and say this:
sn22.85:14.13: Then that householder or householder's son would employ him.
sn22.85:14.14: He would serve him, rising before him, lying down after him, doing whatever was to be done, agreeable in conduct, and pleasing in speech.
sn22.85:14.15: That householder or householder's son would trust him as a friend;
sn22.85:14.16: he would trust him as a companion;
sn22.85:14.17: and he would place confidence in him.
sn22.85:14.19: ‘This householder or householder's son has confidence in me,’ then, knowing him to be alone, he would deprive him of life with a sharp knife.
sn22.85:14.2: Then, friend Yamaka, I will give you a simile for the sake of still further knowledge of this meaning.
sn22.85:14.3: Suppose, friend Yamaka, there were a householder or a householder's son who was rich, with great wealth and great property;
sn22.85:14.5: Then some man would arise who wished him harm, wished him non-benefit, wished him insecurity, and wished to deprive him of life.
sn22.85:14.7: ‘This householder or householder's son is rich, with great wealth and great property;
sn22.85:14.9: it is not easy to overpower him and deprive him of life.
sn22.85:15.1: What do you think, friend Yamaka,
sn22.85:15.10: But although he was a killer, the other did not know:
sn22.85:15.13: “So too, friend, the uninstructed ordinary person, who has not seen the noble ones, is unskilled in the *dhamma* of the noble ones, untrained in the *dhamma* of the noble ones, who has not seen true persons, is unskilled in the *dhamma* of true persons, untrained in the *dhamma* of true persons,
sn22.85:15.14: regards form [*rūpaṁ*] as self, or self as possessing form [*rūpaṁ*]; or form [*rūpaṁ*] as in self, or self as in form [*rūpasmiṁ*].
sn22.85:15.18: he regards *viññāṇa* as self, or self as possessing *viññāṇa*; or *viññāṇa* as in self, or self as in *viññāṇa*.
sn22.85:15.2: when that man approached that householder or householder's son and said:
sn22.85:15.3: ‘I would serve you, *bhante*,’ even then he was a killer.
sn22.85:15.4: But although he was a killer, the other did not know:
sn22.85:15.6: And when he served him, rising before him, lying down after him, doing whatever was to be done, agreeable in conduct, and pleasing in speech, even then he was a killer.
sn22.85:15.7: But although he was a killer, the other did not know:
sn22.85:15.9: And when, knowing him to be alone, he deprived him of life with a sharp knife, even then he was a killer.
sn22.85:16.1: He does not understand as it truly is impermanent form [*aniccaṁ rūpaṁ*] as ‘impermanent form [*aniccaṁ rūpaṁ*].’
sn22.85:16.2: He does not understand as it truly is impermanent *vedanā* [*aniccā vedanā*] as ‘impermanent *vedanā* [*aniccā vedanā*].’
sn22.85:16.3: He does not understand as it truly is impermanent *saññā* [*aniccā saññā*] as ‘impermanent *saññā* [*aniccā saññā*].’
sn22.85:16.4: He does not understand as it truly is impermanent *saṅkhāras* [*aniccā saṅkhārā*] as ‘impermanent *saṅkhāras* [*aniccā saṅkhārā*].’
sn22.85:16.5: He does not understand as it truly is impermanent *viññāṇa* [*aniccaṁ viññāṇaṁ*] as ‘impermanent *viññāṇa* [*aniccaṁ viññāṇaṁ*].’
sn22.85:17.1: He does not understand as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *dukkha* as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *dukkha*.’
sn22.85:17.5: he does not understand as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is *dukkha* as ‘*viññāṇa* that is *dukkha*.’
sn22.85:18.1: He does not understand as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is not-self [*anattaṁ*] as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:18.5: he does not understand as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is not-self [*anattaṁ*] as ‘*viññāṇa* that is not-self [*anattaṁ*].’
sn22.85:19.1: He does not understand as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *saṅkhata* as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *saṅkhata*.’
sn22.85:19.5: he does not understand as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is *saṅkhata* as ‘*viññāṇa* that is *saṅkhata*.’
sn22.85:2.1: Several *bhikkhus* heard that an evil view had arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Yamaka:
sn22.85:2.2: “As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:2.3: Then those *bhikkhus* approached venerable Yamaka; after approaching, they exchanged greetings with venerable Yamaka.
sn22.85:2.4: When they had concluded their courteous and memorable conversation, they sat to one side. Seated to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to venerable Yamaka:
sn22.85:20.1: He does not understand as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is a killer as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is a killer.’
sn22.85:20.2: *Vedanā* that is a killer as ‘*vedanā* that is a killer’ …
sn22.85:20.3: *saññā* that is a killer as ‘*saññā* that is a killer’ …
sn22.85:20.4: he does not understand as it truly is *saṅkhāras* that are killers as ‘*saṅkhāras* that are killers.’
sn22.85:20.5: He does not understand as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is a killer as ‘*viññāṇa* that is a killer.’
sn22.85:21.1: He approaches form [*rūpaṁ*], clings to it, and takes a stand on it as: ‘This is my self.’
sn22.85:21.5: he approaches *viññāṇa*, clings to it, and takes a stand on it as: ‘This is my self.’
sn22.85:21.6: These five aggregates subject to *upādāna*, approached and taken up by him, lead for a long time to his non-benefit and *dukkha*.
sn22.85:22.1: But, friend, the instructed noble disciple, who has seen the noble ones … well trained in the *dhamma* of true persons,
sn22.85:22.2: does not regard form [*rūpaṁ*] as self, or self as possessing form [*rūpaṁ*]; or form [*rūpaṁ*] as in self, or self as in form [*rūpasmiṁ*].
sn22.85:22.6: he does not regard *viññāṇa* as self, or self as possessing *viññāṇa*;
sn22.85:22.7: or *viññāṇa* as in self, or self as in *viññāṇa*.
sn22.85:23.1: He understands as it truly is impermanent form [*aniccaṁ rūpaṁ*] as ‘impermanent form [*aniccaṁ rūpaṁ*].’
sn22.85:23.5: he understands as it truly is impermanent *viññāṇa* [*aniccaṁ viññāṇaṁ*] as ‘impermanent *viññāṇa* [*aniccaṁ viññāṇaṁ*].’
sn22.85:24.1: He understands as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *dukkha* as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *dukkha*.’
sn22.85:24.5: he understands as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is *dukkha* as ‘*viññāṇa* that is *dukkha*.’
sn22.85:25.1: He understands as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is not-self [*anattaṁ*] as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:25.5: he understands as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is not-self [*anattaṁ*] as ‘*viññāṇa* that is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:26.1: He understands as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *saṅkhata* as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is *saṅkhata*.’
sn22.85:26.5: he understands as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is *saṅkhata* as ‘*viññāṇa* that is *saṅkhata*.’
sn22.85:27.1: He understands as it truly is form [*rūpaṁ*] that is a killer as ‘form [*rūpaṁ*] that is a killer.’
sn22.85:27.4: he understands as it truly is *saṅkhāras* that are killers as ‘*saṅkhāras* that are killers.’
sn22.85:27.5: He understands as it truly is *viññāṇa* that is a killer as ‘*viññāṇa* that is a killer.’
sn22.85:28.1: He does not approach form [*rūpaṁ*], does not cling to it, and does not take a stand on it as: ‘This is my self.’
sn22.85:28.5: he does not approach *viññāṇa*, does not cling to it, and does not take a stand on it as: ‘This is my self.’
sn22.85:28.6: These five aggregates subject to *upādāna*, not approached and not taken up by him, lead for a long time to his benefit and *sukha*.”
sn22.85:28.7: “So it is, friend Sāriputta, for venerable ones who have such compassionate fellow practitioners of the holy life, who desire their welfare, who advise and instruct them.
sn22.85:28.8: And having heard this teaching of *dhamma* from venerable Sāriputta, my mind [*cittaṁ*] has been liberated from the *āsavas* without *upādāna*.”
sn22.85:3.1: “Is it true, friend Yamaka, that such an evil view has arisen in you:
sn22.85:3.2: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:3.3: “Yes, friends, as I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.85:3.4: ‘a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.’”
sn22.85:4.1: “Do not speak so, friend Yamaka. Do not misrepresent the *Bhagavā*. It is not *sādhu* to misrepresent the *Bhagavā*. The *Bhagavā* would not speak thus:
sn22.85:4.2: ‘a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.’”
sn22.85:4.3: But although venerable Yamaka was spoken to in this way by those *bhikkhus*, still he continued to assert that same evil view, clinging to it firmly, holding to it tenaciously:
sn22.85:4.4: “As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:5.1: When those *bhikkhus* could not detach venerable Yamaka from that evil view, they rose from their seats and approached venerable Sāriputta; after approaching, they said this to venerable Sāriputta:
sn22.85:5.2: “Friend Sāriputta, an evil view has arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Yamaka:
sn22.85:5.3: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.’
sn22.85:5.4: *Sādhu*, may venerable Sāriputta approach the *bhikkhu* Yamaka out of compassion.”
sn22.85:5.6: Then, in the evening, venerable Sāriputta rose from retreat and approached venerable Yamaka; after approaching, he exchanged greetings with venerable Yamaka … seated to one side, venerable Sāriputta said this to venerable Yamaka:
sn22.85:6.1: “Is it true, friend Yamaka, that such an evil view has arisen in you:
sn22.85:6.2: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:6.3: “Yes, friend, as I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, a *bhikkhu* with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated and perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:7.11: one understands: ‘There is no more for this state of being.’
sn22.85:8.1: What do you think, friend Yamaka,
sn22.85:8.2: do you regard form [*rūpaṁ*] as the Tathāgata?”
sn22.85:8.8: do you regard *viññāṇa* as the Tathāgata?”
sn22.85:9.12: do you regard the Tathāgata as being in *viññāṇa*?”
sn22.85:9.14: “Do you regard the Tathāgata as apart from *viññāṇa*?”
sn22.85:9.2: do you regard the Tathāgata as being in form [*rūpasmiṁ*]?”
sn22.85:9.4: “Do you regard the Tathāgata as apart from form [*rūpā*]?”
